name: Silkenweb
description: Fine-grained signals built on futures-signals, no virtual DOM

description.value: A library for writing reactive single-page web apps using fine-grained signals and plain Rust syntax, with no virtual DOM
description.comment: Repo tagline: 'A library for writing reactive single page web apps'.

license.comment: Dual-licensed 'MIT OR Apache-2.0' across all published versions.

documentation.comment: API docs on docs.rs; repo also ships an mdbook guide under packages/book. crates.io documentation field is unset.

latest-stable-version.comment: Latest release as of 2026-07-19; still pre-1.0.

maintenance-status.comment: Last feature release (0.10.0) was 2025-05-24; recent commits (through April 2026) are dependency (dependabot) bumps rather than feature work. Solo maintainer.

maturity.comment: Feature-complete for SPA/SSR but still 0.x (0.10.0) with breaking changes between minors and no widely documented production users.

corporate-foundation-backing.comment: Solo/community project maintained primarily by Simon Bourne; no corporate or foundation backing.

reactivity-model.comment: Fine-grained reactivity built on futures-signals; 'No VDOM. Fine grained reactivity using signals to minimize DOM API calls.'

component-model.comment: Components are plain Rust functions that build and return elements; there is also a component wrapper for web-component slots.

view-authoring.comment: Views are written with a plain-Rust builder API (e.g. div().child(button().on_click(inc).text("+"))), deliberately avoiding a macro DSL. An inline_html! macro and a css! macro exist for HTML/CSS embedding.

fine-grained-updates.comment: Signal updates target the exact DOM nodes affected; no diffing.

async-await-in-components.comment: Signals are built on futures; async data loading is supported (async-http-request example, silkenweb::task spawn helpers). No dedicated suspense primitive.

global-state-solution.comment: Shared state is handled via futures-signals Mutables/signals passed through the app; no built-in context provide/inject.

primary-build-tool.comment: Quick-start uses Trunk; wasm-pack is used for browser tests.

official-cli.comment: No first-party scaffolding/dev-server CLI; relies on Trunk.

dev-server-hmr.comment: Dev loop via Trunk, which provides auto-rebuild + browser live reload; no view/RSX hot reload.

minimal-wasm-bundle-size.comment: No documented, reproducible minimal hello-world .wasm measurement under a consistent opt profile; omitted per RESEARCH.md rather than guessing.

js-interop.comment: Built on wasm-bindgen and web-sys; element/event wrappers surface web-sys underneath.

js-interop-ergonomics.comment: Standard wasm-bindgen/web-sys interop with the usual boilerplate; the builder API wraps common DOM/event needs but dropping to raw web APIs is unremarkable.

no-std-minimal-runtime.comment: Depends on std and web-sys; no no_std mode.

client-side-rendering-spa.comment: Primary target is client-rendered single-page web apps.

server-side-rendering.comment: 'Server side rendering with hydration' plus progressive enhancement.

static-site-generation.comment: Supports static site generation and compile-time pre-rendering.

hydration.comment: SSR output is hydrated on the client (progressive enhancement); no documented islands or streaming hydration modes.

server-functions.comment: No integrated isomorphic server-function macro; the README points to the separate Arpy library for typed client/server RPC.

streaming-ssr.comment: No documented streaming SSR; SSR renders to a complete HTML string.

backend-integration.comment: Axum is demonstrated (htmx-axum, client-server examples); SSR renders to a string so it is largely server-agnostic.

isomorphic-routing.comment: Built-in router module works on both client and server.

router-name.comment: Built-in module rather than a separate crate; exposes url_path() as a signal.

nested-routes.comment: Router exposes the URL path as a signal that the app matches manually; no built-in nested-layout routing construct.

dynamic-route-params.comment: Path components and query maps are accessible (with_args/arg1/arg2, query_map()), so parameterized routes are supported via manual matching.

typed-routes.comment: Routes are string/path-component matched at runtime, not derive/enum type-checked.

route-guards.comment: No built-in navigation-guard/redirect-hook API; guards would be implemented manually in the path signal handler.

lazy-code-split-routes.comment: No documented per-route code splitting / lazy loading.

styling-approach.comment: CSS Modules provide scoping (integrated with SSR/hydration); a css! macro embeds/imports CSS in Rust; a tailwind example is included.

scoped-styles.comment: CSS scoping via CSS Modules so styles do not leak globally.

component-library-ecosystem.comment: A first-party silkenweb-bootstrap component kit exists; otherwise essentially none. Rated 2 (roughly one kit).

forms-inputs.comment: Inputs are wired to signals (value/event handlers), i.e. controlled; no dedicated form-helper or two-way-binding abstraction.

svg-canvas-support.comment: SVG elements are available in silkenweb::elements::svg and a canvas example is included.

suspense-loading-ui.comment: No declarative suspense/loading-boundary primitive documented; async loading states are handled manually via signals.

documentation-quality.comment: README overview, an mdbook guide (packages/book), docs.rs API docs, and 28 examples; adequate but not an extensive tutorial series.

api-ergonomics.comment: Idiomatic plain-Rust builder API with no macro DSL; functional and readable but signal wiring adds some verbosity.

compile-times.comment: Builder-API design avoids heavy view proc-macros, so compile times are middling relative to macro-heavy peers; no published benchmark, hence a conservative 'moderate'.

examples-count.comment: 28 example directories in the official repo (>20).

interactive-playground.comment: No hosted online playground/REPL found; only the repo examples.

browser-devtools.comment: No dedicated devtools/inspector beyond generic browser tools.

testing-support.comment: Testing example uses wasm-bindgen-test with a first-party silkenweb-test BrowserTest harness (mount + render, assert on DOM), run via wasm-pack test.

crates-io-downloads.comment: All-time downloads of the silkenweb crate as of 2026-07-19.

contributors.comment: GitHub contributors API count on 2026-07-19; effectively solo-maintained.

community-size.comment: Single primary maintainer, ~4 contributors, 287 stars; no large public chat community.

ecosystem-crates.comment: Router is in-tree; a bootstrap component kit and Arpy (RPC) exist, but the surrounding third-party ecosystem is small.

notable-production-users.comment: No widely documented production/company users found.

meta-framework-full-stack-kit.comment: No named meta-framework; full-stack RPC is provided out-of-tree via the separate Arpy library.
